name: ETLAS-2 Main Trial Extract
population_or_context: Patients with cerebral small vessel disease and prior stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age about 68 years.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled double-blind parallel phase II trial of tadalafil 20 mg daily for 3 months versus placebo, with feasibility as the primary outcome and cognition, imaging markers, blood pressure, and adverse events as secondary outcomes.
population_profile: CSVD patients with previous stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age 68.0 years; not healthy adults and not the target population of mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
indication_context: Experimental off-label CSVD disease-modification/vascular cognitive impairment trial.
study_design: Randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind, parallel phase II clinical trial.
safety_findings: Adverse events occurred more often with tadalafil than placebo in the trial abstract, and adherence to tadalafil was lower than placebo.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect: tadalafil-specific and human with repeated dosing, but older post-stroke/CSVD population rather than healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Feasibility-primary phase II trial; indirect population; not designed to test healthy-adult cognitive enhancement.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; no cross-agent PDE5 transfer is needed, but population, dose, duration, indication, comparator, and endpoint limits still constrain application to mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
